evidence_quote: Lord Walder had ordered the slaughter of the Starks at Roslin's wedding; watches the slaughter 'greedily' from his throne; mocks Robb: 'Seems we killed some of your men, Your Grace.'
rationale: Lord Walder ordered the slaughter and watched it from his throne without personally executing anyone.

evidence_quote: Additional context: Tywin Lannister countenanced it (confidence_tier=2); 'Jaime Lannister sends his regards' spoken by the killer.
rationale: Tywin countenanced the massacre but did not directly order or execute it; 'Jaime Lannister sends his regards' implies Lannister foreknowledge at one remove.

evidence_quote: 'Lame Lothar who had plotted it out with Roose Bolton'; Merrett names him as one of the actual plotters: 'his father, Ryman, Bolton, Lothar, Bastard Walder.'
rationale: Lothar co-plotted the massacre with Roose Bolton but is not identified as a personal executioner.

evidence_quote: 'A man in dark armor and a pale pink cloak spotted with blood steps up and says "Jaime Lannister sends his regards." He thrusts his longsword through Robb's heart and twists.'
rationale: Roose personally thrust his longsword through Robb's heart, physically executing the killing blow.

evidence_quote: 'Ser Ryman kills Dacey Mormont — He buries his axe head in her stomach.'
rationale: Ser Ryman personally killed Dacey Mormont with an axe during the massacre.

evidence_quote: 'Lucas Blackwood is cut down — Killed by Ser Hosteen Frey.'
rationale: Ser Hosteen personally cut down Lucas Blackwood during the slaughter.

evidence_quote: Additional context: 'Raymund Frey cut Catelyn's throat'; source: 'Someone grabs her scalp and cuts her throat.'
rationale: Raymund Frey cut Catelyn Stark's throat, as identified in the canonical additional context.

evidence_quote: 'Black Walder | Hamstrings | One of the Vances | During the massacre.'
rationale: Black Walder personally hamstrung one of the Vances during the massacre.

evidence_quote: 'Musicians in the gallery have crossbows instead of instruments'; crossbow bolts kill Donnel Locke, Owen Norrey, and half a dozen others.
rationale: Frey crossbowmen in the musicians' gallery shot Robb, Catelyn, Smalljon Umber, and many other northern guests.

evidence_quote: Additional context: 'Bolton men-at-arms and Frey crossbowmen killed numerous northmen in the camps'; soldiers wear pale pink cloaks with the flayed-man badge.
rationale: Bolton men-at-arms participated in killing northern soldiers in the camps during the massacre.

evidence_quote: 'A crossbow bolt sprouts from his side beneath the shoulder. A second pierces his leg. He falls… He thrusts his longsword through Robb's heart and twists.'
rationale: Robb was shot twice by crossbow bolts and then stabbed through the heart by Roose Bolton.

evidence_quote: 'She is running toward Robb when something hits her in the small of the back; she falls to the floor… Someone grabs her scalp and cuts her throat.'
rationale: Catelyn was shot in the back and ultimately had her throat cut by Raymund Frey.

evidence_quote: 'Ser Ryman kills Dacey Mormont — He buries his axe head in her stomach.'
rationale: Dacey Mormont was killed by Ser Ryman Frey's axe blow to the stomach.

evidence_quote: Additional context: 'The Red Wedding took place during Edmure Tully's wedding feast at the Twins.'
rationale: The Red Wedding took place inside the great hall of the Twins during Edmure Tully's wedding feast.

evidence_quote: Lady Olenna fussed with Sansa's hair net (tucking, straightening); Sansa finds one black amethyst missing, with only a dark smudge in the socket, and is seized by terror suspecting the stone was used in Joffrey's death.
rationale: Lady Olenna physically extracted the Strangler-laced amethyst stone from Sansa's hair net while fussing with it before the feast, then introduced the poison into Joffrey's wine cup.

evidence_quote: Littlefinger reveals his orchestration — 'All he did he did at my behest'; Dontos was always his catspaw, recruited after Sansa saved Dontos's life at Joffrey's tourney.
rationale: Littlefinger conceived and orchestrated the entire assassination plot — procuring the poisoned hair net, recruiting Dontos as catspaw, and engineering Sansa's escape as cover — without personally executing the killing.

evidence_quote: Littlefinger explains Dontos was always his catspaw; Dontos guided Sansa through the escape and arranged the hair net, insisting to Sansa that the stones were 'black amethysts' and that 'Joffrey choked on his pigeon pie.'
rationale: Ser Dontos physically delivered the poisoned hair net to Sansa and ensured she wore it to the feast, forming an essential link in the execution chain even if he was unaware of its lethal purpose.

evidence_quote: Pulling the silver hair net free from her auburn hair, she finds one black amethyst missing, with only a dark smudge in the socket; she is seized by terror, suspecting the stone was used in Joffrey's death.
rationale: Sansa unwittingly served as the courier of the poisoned hair net, wearing it to the feast and thereby placing the Strangler within Olenna's reach — an instrumental but entirely unknowing role.

evidence_quote: Joffrey dies — His eyes meet Tyrion's. He lifts a hand toward Tyrion. A fearful thin sound stops. Tommen screams and cries. Cersei declares 'My son was poisoned.'
rationale: Joffrey Baratheon was the target of the poisoning and died at his own wedding feast after drinking from the wine cup into which the Strangler had been introduced.

evidence_quote: The feast, Joffrey's humiliation of Tyrion as cupbearer, and Joffrey's death all occur at the wedding banquet in King's Landing, centered at the Red Keep.
rationale: The wedding feast at which Joffrey was poisoned took place at the Red Keep in King's Landing.

evidence_quote: Catelyn swears on Ned's grave that 'Stannis killed Renly through sorcery'; the shadow bore Stannis's face and was sustained by his life force.
rationale: Stannis's life force was used to create the shadow assassin, making him the instigating power behind the killing, though he did not personally wield the blade.

evidence_quote: Renly Baratheon was killed in his tent by a shadow creature created by Melisandre using Stannis's life force.
rationale: Melisandre performed the shadowbinding ritual that created and dispatched the shadow creature; she is the functional executor of the assassination.

evidence_quote: Renly says 'Cold' and his gorget parts like cheesecloth; blood gushes from his throat; he collapses into Brienne's arms.
rationale: Renly was killed when the shadow's sword parted his gorget and blood gushed from his throat.

evidence_quote: A gust of wind opens the tent door; the shadow lifts a sword — the scene is set in Renly's command tent outside Storm's End.
rationale: The assassination occurred inside Renly's tent at his camp near Storm's End.

evidence_quote: Cersei orders Ned's arrest — Commands Ser Barristan to seize 'this traitor.'
rationale: Ned is the primary target seized as a traitor and imprisoned in the throne room.

evidence_quote: Cersei orders Ned's arrest — Commands Ser Barristan to seize 'this traitor.'
rationale: Cersei explicitly issues the arrest order but does not personally seize Ned.

evidence_quote: Joffrey screams for killing — 'Kill him! Kill all of them, I command it!'
rationale: Joffrey, as the newly declared king, commands the killing of Ned and his men, reinforcing Cersei's order with his own royal authority.

evidence_quote: Littlefinger's final betrayal — Slides Ned's dagger from its sheath and holds it under Ned's chin, saying 'I did warn you not to trust me, you know.'
rationale: Littlefinger personally draws Ned's own dagger and holds it to his throat, physically completing the betrayal and securing the arrest.

evidence_quote: Gold cloaks betray Ned — Instead of arresting Cersei, a gold cloak drives a spear through Tomard's back, killing him. Janos Slynt slashes Varly's throat. Cayn fights back briefly but is cut down by the Hound.
rationale: Lannister soldiers and the turned City Watch (gold cloaks) physically execute the arrest, slaughtering Ned's escort in the throne room.

evidence_quote: Syrio Forel killed protecting Arya during the arrest — cut down by Lannister swords.
rationale: Syrio is killed by Lannister men during the broader arrest operation while defending Arya Stark, making him a secondary victim of the same event.

evidence_quote: Eddard Stark was arrested in the throne room by Lannister soldiers and the City Watch.
rationale: The arrest and surrounding slaughter take place in the throne room of the Red Keep.

evidence_quote: Ser Ilyn Payne | executes | Eddard Stark | Draws Ice and carries out the beheading
rationale: Eddard Stark was beheaded by Ser Ilyn Payne on Joffrey's order.

evidence_quote: Joffrey commands Ser Ilyn Payne to bring him Eddard's head
rationale: Joffrey explicitly commanded Ser Ilyn Payne to bring him Eddard's head, overruling both Cersei and Sansa.

evidence_quote: Ser Ilyn Payne | executes | Eddard Stark | Draws Ice and carries out the beheading
rationale: Ser Ilyn Payne physically carried out the beheading using Ice.

evidence_quote: Ser Ilyn Payne | executes | Eddard Stark | Draws Ice and carries out the beheading
rationale: The Stark greatsword Ice was the named weapon used to perform the beheading.

evidence_quote: Eddard declares himself a traitor... Joffrey commands Ser Ilyn Payne... [at the Great Sept of Baelor]
rationale: The execution took place publicly at the Great Sept of Baelor, where Eddard made his confession.

evidence_quote: Steps from shadows with crossbow; explains his actions are for the realm and children; wants to undo Kevan's stabilizing work
rationale: Varys personally shot Kevan Lannister with a crossbow and orchestrated the killing of Pycelle via his little-bird child agents, then revealed himself as the executor.

evidence_quote: Pycelle's head bashed in at his table, blood and brain pooled around him
rationale: Pycelle was found murdered in his chambers, skull bashed in, before Kevan was shot.

evidence_quote: A crossbow bolt slams into his chest between the ribs
rationale: Kevan was shot through the chest with a crossbow bolt fired by Varys while investigating Pycelle's murder.

evidence_quote: Aegon raises his banner; monologue about Aegon's fitness to rule
rationale: Varys explicitly states he acts to benefit Aegon's claim and ease his path to the throne, making Aegon the instigating beneficiary, though no direct order from Aegon is shown.

evidence_quote: Kevan crosses to Pycelle's chambers — castle alien under snow
rationale: Both murders occur within the Red Keep — Pycelle in his chambers and Kevan after crossing to find him there.

evidence_quote: Daenerys acquired the Unsullied by pretending to trade a dragon, then commanded 'Dracarys' — her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers while the Unsullied killed their former masters.
rationale: Daenerys orchestrated the entire deception and gave the command 'Dracarys' to trigger the attack, but did not personally execute the killing.

evidence_quote: Grey Worm led the Unsullied assault.
rationale: Grey Worm led the Unsullied in the physical assault on the city and its slavemasters.

evidence_quote: Her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers; Drogon is the dragon most explicitly named in connection with the 'Dracarys' command.
rationale: Drogon was the primary dragon who burned Kraznys mo Nakloz upon Daenerys's 'Dracarys' command, making him the named instrument of the killing.

evidence_quote: Her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers while the Unsullied killed their former masters and took the city.
rationale: Rhaegal participated in the burning of Astapor alongside Drogon and Viserion, serving as a weapon wielded by Daenerys's command.

evidence_quote: Her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers while the Unsullied killed their former masters and took the city.
rationale: Viserion participated in the sack of Astapor as one of Daenerys's three dragons, wielded collectively in the assault.

evidence_quote: Her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers.
rationale: Kraznys mo Nakloz was the first and most prominent victim, burned alive by Drogon when Daenerys said 'Dracarys' while he still held the whip.

evidence_quote: The Unsullied (now freed) killed their former masters and took the city.
rationale: The Good Masters of Astapor were the collective victims of the sack — killed by dragon fire and the freed Unsullied.

evidence_quote: Daenerys acquired the Unsullied by pretending to trade a dragon, then commanded 'Dracarys' — her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers while the Unsullied killed their former masters and took the city.
rationale: The sack took place within Astapor itself, specifically in the Plaza of Punishment where the transaction occurred.

evidence_quote: Dany assembles her bloodriders and captains; plans a surprise attack at an hour past midnight... Dany stays in her pavilion with her maids while Whitebeard and Belwas guard.
rationale: She planned and ordered the entire attack but explicitly remained in her pavilion while her forces fought.

evidence_quote: He is first of her Queensguard, commander of her army; Jorah wants Daario held under guard — implying he was coordinating the operation.
rationale: As commander of her army he directed forces in the field, though the source does not explicitly describe his personal battlefield actions during this engagement.

evidence_quote: Unsullied to strike from right and left; she speaks with Grey Worm about the coming battle.
rationale: He commanded the Unsullied, the primary assault force assigned to strike from right and left flanks.

evidence_quote: Daario pledges the Stormcrows to Dany; assigns Stormcrows to attack the Yunkish rear.
rationale: He turned the Stormcrows to Dany's side and led them in attacking the Yunkish rear during the battle.

evidence_quote: Whitebeard and Belwas guard [Dany's pavilion during the battle].
rationale: He was part of Dany's military force during the battle but his explicit role was guarding the pavilion rather than leading an assault element.

evidence_quote: The Second Sons will be drunk on the gifted wine... Mero fled; ~200 Yunkai'i died.
rationale: As captain of the Second Sons fighting for Yunkai, his forces were defeated (drunk, fought poorly) and he himself fled — the defending side received the action.

evidence_quote: Daenerys attacked Yunkai at night with Unsullied, Dothraki, and the turned Stormcrows.
rationale: The battle took place at the city of Yunkai, which gives the engagement its name.

evidence_quote: Tyrion decides to lead the sortie himself — 'This is madness, but sooner madness than defeat.'
rationale: Tyrion personally directed the wildfire trap, raised the chain, and physically led the sortie out the gates to repel Stannis's landing parties.

evidence_quote: Tywin Lannister arrived with Tyrell forces to relieve the city (additional context); Tyrion compares Stannis to Lord Tywin — both command from the reserve/rear.
rationale: Tywin commanded the relief army (including Tyrell forces) that broke the siege, directing forces from a command position rather than personally fighting.

evidence_quote: Tyrion compares Stannis to Lord Tywin — both command from the reserve/rear; Stannis lacks Robert's thirst for battle.
rationale: Stannis ordered and directed the assault on King's Landing but commanded from the reserve/rear rather than personally executing the attack.

evidence_quote: Cersei orders executions — Commands Ser Ilyn to behead the would-be deserters and put their heads on pikes outside the stables.
rationale: Cersei managed the Red Keep's internal defenses during the battle, ordering executions of would-be deserters and directing the Kettleblack brothers, though she did not personally fight.

evidence_quote: Davos Seaworth led Stannis fleet (additional context); Stannis likely retains thirty to forty galleys — those that escaped the wildfire Davos had led into.
rationale: Davos physically led Stannis's fleet in the naval assault through Blackwater Bay, personally executing the seaborne attack.

evidence_quote: Mace Tyrell reinforced via Renly's former host (additional context); Ser Loras Tyrell led the vanguard of the relief force.
rationale: Mace Tyrell commanded the Tyrell forces that joined Tywin's relief army, directing his host rather than personally leading the vanguard charge.

evidence_quote: Ser Loras Tyrell (vanguard of relief force) (additional context).
rationale: Ser Loras personally led the vanguard of the relief force, physically executing the charge that broke Stannis's besieging army.

evidence_quote: Ser Mandon backs him up; but Tyrion recalls Jaime's assessment that he's dangerous but not a leader of men — and later Ser Mandon Moore attacked Tyrion (additional context).
rationale: Ser Mandon Moore fought in the sortie alongside Tyrion and then treacherously attacked Tyrion during the battle, making him a direct physical actor in the engagement.

evidence_quote: Eight or more ships have landed men under the city walls; Mud Gate under attack, ram at King's Gate.
rationale: The battle was fought at King's Landing and in Blackwater Bay, which served as both the naval theater and the besieged city.
